finding: Evidence validator linking splits literal task-card validator commands on commas even when the comma appears inside a quoted command argument, causing taskflow pre-close to report stale evidence for a command that already passed.
expectedBehavior: Evidence run/add and taskflow pre-close should preserve a task-declared literal validator command as one validator identity, including quoted arguments that contain commas.
evidenceOrRepro: During TASK-RFT-0096, the declared candidate-rank validator contained a --goal string with commas. `node atm.mjs evidence run ... --validators "<literal command>"` and command auto-link recorded split validation passes such as `schema validation` and `normalized model output`, so pre-close kept reporting ATM_TASKFLOW_PRECLOSE_STALE_EVIDENCE until the command run was relinked through a JSON command-runs array.
followUp: Replace CSV-only validator parsing with quote-aware parsing or prefer JSON-array validator input for literal command validators, then add a regression where a task-card declared validator contains quoted commas.
